extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Adam won from a late two-man break with twin brother Simon, beating the peloton by 20 seconds on the uphill finish
tactical_patterns: Yates brothers established 20-second gap in closing miles despite narrow technical roads favoring peloton control, exploiting hesitation around tempo responsibility
Jumbo-Visma maintained chase tempo but couldn't close despite having Pogacar's teammate Adam Yates ahead, revealing GC protection prioritized over stage control
Early third-category climb provoked aggressive tempo splits from Encom/Guegard, signaling teams willing to fragment field despite 182km remaining on opening stage

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Outsprinted twin brother Simon from two-up despite latter doing more pacemaking, UAE teammate Pogacar in chase negated cooperation obligation
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Did majority of pulling in winning break but couldn't contest sprint, suggests weaker top-end power than twin brother
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: Victory salute unaware of front break indicates UAE team radio dysfunction or tactical miscommunication on stage one
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: Jumbo maintained chase but allowed 20-second loss on opening stage, suggesting calculated acceptance of time gaps on hard finishes
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: Listed as dropped 17 seconds with 1 mile remaining despite Jumbo tempo control, signals poor opening form for key domestique
